summary: Matched additional ground/stage code, centered on implementing Ground_801C0C2C and tightening related type/prototype information. The PR also removed bogus parameters from gmMainLib_8015ED8C, gm_80164ABC, and un_8031C2EC, converted StageInfo callback slots x90/x94 from integers to function pointers, corrected x9C to u32, and adjusted call sites in ground and item code accordingly.
what_changed: Implemented previously placeholder-documented Ground_801C0C2C in src/melee/gr/ground.c.

Ground_801C0C2C now checks stage_info.unk8C.b6/b7, gets a fighter object via Ground_801C57A4, skips inactive/invalid fighters with ftLib_8008701C, reads fighter position with ftLib_80086644, scans ground point ranges 0x99..0xB2 and 0xBD..0xC6 with Ground_801C2D24, applies rectangular bounds from stage_info.x70C/x710 and x718/x71C, and stores found IDs in stage_info.x714/x720 or -1.

Ground_801C0C2C also handles Bob-omb rain spawning: when gm_8016B238 is true and gm_8016AEDC timing exceeds thresholds, it copies Ground_803B7DEC into a local BobOmbRain, fills x8_vec from Ground_801C0A70, and calls it_8026BE84.
Changed Ground_803B7DEC from an int array to a typed BobOmbRain constant with six scalar initializers, matching its later use as a BobOmbRain template.
Changed StageInfo.x90 and StageInfo.x94 from s32 fields to callback pointers of type bool (*)(Vec3*, int), matching their use as nullable filters in Ground_801C0C2C.
Changed StageInfo.x9C from s32 to u32, matching its use as a frame/timer comparison value against gm_8016AEDC.
Initialized stage_info.x90 and x94 with NULL instead of integer 0 in Ground_801BFFB0.
Changed Ground_801C3D44 and Ground_801C3DB4 first parameters from s32 to void* in both implementation and header, while preserving their stage search state setup behavior.
Removed unused/bogus argument from gmMainLib_8015ED8C: prototype and implementation now take void and return a u16 pointer into gmMainLib_804D3EE0->thing.
Removed unused/bogus argument from gm_80164ABC: it now takes void and calls gmMainLib_8015ED8C() directly to test the unlockable-character bitfield.
Updated call sites in gm_80164840, Ground_801C24F8, it_8027A4D4, and it_8027A9B8 to call gmMainLib_8015ED8C() or gm_80164ABC() without fake arguments/casts.
Removed the now-unused forward declaration typedef for gmMainLib_8015ED8C_arg0_t from gm/forward.h.
Changed un_8031C2EC prototype to take void and updated Ground_801C5878 to call un_8031C2EC() without passing &stage_info.
Adjusted Ground_ApplyStageBackgroundColor so GET_FOG(stage_info.x12C) is only evaluated after checking stage_info.x12C != NULL, using assignment inside the condition.

Changed Ground_801C2090 behavior in the diff from assigning *color into fog->color to assigning fog->color into *color, making the function read out the fog color when a valid color pointer is supplied.
smart_moves: Replaced fake function parameters and casts with void prototypes once the callees were shown not to consume arguments, reducing misleading type dependencies and simplifying all call sites.

Promoted StageInfo.x90/x94 to explicit callback types instead of leaving them as integer placeholders; this made the newly matched Ground_801C0C2C logic type-check naturally when invoking nullable callbacks.
Used a typed BobOmbRain constant instead of a raw int array, which clarified the data object’s real role and supported direct struct-copy initialization before overriding x8_vec.
Added ft/ftlib.h only when Ground_801C0C2C needed ftLib_8008701C and ftLib_80086644, keeping includes evidence-based.
Preserved match-sensitive boolean temporaries and stepwise bounds checks in Ground_801C0C2C rather than collapsing them into a single expression, which is often necessary for MWCC codegen.
Used NULL for pointer/callback fields in stage_info initialization, making pointer semantics explicit without changing runtime value.
Removed the obsolete gmMainLib_8015ED8C_arg0_t forward declaration after eliminating the only visible API need for that placeholder type.
decomp_lessons: When a decompiled function accepts an argument that is never used, verify whether callers are carrying fake context solely to satisfy an incorrect prototype; converting to void can unlock cleaner matches across game-mode, item, and stage code.
StageInfo unknown integer fields may actually be function callbacks. Evidence includes NULL initialization plus guarded call syntax such as stage_info.x90 == NULL || stage_info.x90(&pos, id).
Raw data arrays should be revisited when code starts copying them into locals of a known struct type; a typed const object can both document intent and match codegen.
For matching, avoid over-simplifying nested boolean flows in spatial tests. Temporary bools like var_r4_2, var_r3_2, and var_r0_2 may mirror the original compiler’s branch structure.
Header corrections should be propagated immediately to all call sites to eliminate casts that were only compensating for bad prototypes.
For null-sensitive macros such as GET_FOG, matching and safety can require ordering the object null check before macro evaluation.
Field signedness matters for frame/timer comparisons; x9C was corrected to u32 because Ground_801C0C2C compares and subtracts timing values from gm_8016AEDC.
naming_conventions: Ground functions retain address-based names such as Ground_801C0C2C, Ground_801C3D44, and Ground_801C3DB4 when semantic names are not yet known.
Global typed data keeps address-based symbol naming, e.g. Ground_803B7DEC, but the type BobOmbRain communicates recovered semantics.
Unknown StageInfo fields remain offset-style names such as x90, x94, x9C, x70C, x710, x714, x718, x71C, and x720, with improved C types where known.
Game-mode and utility functions keep address-based names but use void prototypes when no parameters are supported: gm_80164ABC(void), gmMainLib_8015ED8C(void), and un_8031C2EC(void).
Local variables in the matched function preserve compiler/reverse-engineering style names like sp50, sp44, sp38, sp2C, var_r0_2, var_r3_2, and var_r4_2, likely to preserve matching behavior.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Implemented Ground_801C0C2C with explicit stack Vec3 locals and a stack BobOmbRain local copied from Ground_803B7DEC, matching the pattern of copying a template struct and then writing a vector field before item spawn.
Kept loop bounds as numeric constants 0x99 to 0xB3 and 0xBD to 0xC7, preserving exact control-flow shape for two ground-point searches.
Used separate min/max variables computed as signed positive/negative halves from stage_info extents, which likely helps reproduce floating-point operation order.
Maintained chained boolean temporaries for rectangle membership instead of combining comparisons, which is a common MWCC matching tactic.
Changed function prototypes to void rather than passing dummy pointers; this removes unnecessary register setup at call sites and can be necessary for exact assembly matches.
Reordered fog retrieval into `if (stage_info.x12C != NULL && (fog = GET_FOG(stage_info.x12C)) != NULL)` to control evaluation order and generated branches.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename Ground_801C0C2C or assign semantic names to StageInfo.x90/x94 callbacks.
Search for callers of Ground_801C3D44 and Ground_801C3DB4 to determine the real type of their first void* argument.
Search for later changes around Ground_801C2090 to confirm whether it is intended to get fog color rather than set fog color.
Investigate BobOmbRain struct definition and item spawn path it_8026BE84 for field names beyond x8_vec.
Check later PRs for semantic names for stage_info.x70C/x710/x714/x718/x71C/x720 and the two ground-point ranges 0x99..0xB2 and 0xBD..0xC6.
